title: [Hooks] Stop hook silently allows-exit when session state file disappears mid-run despite unresolved phases
summary: The Claude Code stop hook can return success and allow exit when a previously valid session state file disappears during a non-terminal orchestration run. The implementation process should add recovery or loud failure before the missing-session-file branch silently exits, plus audit logging around session state removal/deactivation paths.
acceptanceFocus: Missing session state file for a hook-bound non-terminal run must not silently approve exit.
When the hook can safely recover run identity and run directory, it should recreate or repair the session binding and continue blocking orchestration.
When the hook cannot safely recover an active run but has evidence that recovery is required, it should fail visibly/non-zero with a clear diagnostic.
Legitimate no-session/no-run cases must keep their existing allow-exit behavior.
Session state removal or deactivation paths touched by the fix should emit auditable reason metadata or logs.
qualityGates: Regression tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation.
SDK test suite passes or any pre-existing unrelated failures are documented with evidence.
Reviewer approves recovery semantics and compatibility with existing hook/session behavior.
nonGoals: Do not fix unrelated resume path issues from #138 except where needed for shared recovery helpers.
Do not change broad orchestration lifecycle semantics outside the stop-hook/session binding path.
Do not introduce shell-oriented workflow tasks in this repository process.
